codeReview.summary: All six YPM_HOST→VIRTUAL_HOST replacements were made correctly across the two files. The diff is precise and surgical: README.md lines 32, 50 (two occurrences), 52, 53, 260, and 270 were updated; traefik-proxy/README.md line 24 was updated. No surrounding text or formatting was altered. Post-commit verification confirms zero YPM_HOST occurrences remain in either file, and VIRTUAL_HOST appears at all expected locations (7 occurrences in README.md, 1 in traefik-proxy/README.md). All repository validators (pytest, ruff check, ruff format, mypy) passed. The feature's expectedBehavior and verificationSteps are fully satisfied.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `frontend-worker` skill was assigned to a pure documentation (markdown) task, causing the worker to deviate from steps 4 (TDD/Vitest) and 6 (frontend build validators). The deviations were correct and justified, but the skill mismatch added noise to the transcript and required the worker to explicitly explain why frontend procedures didn't apply. A documentation-specific skill (or a note in `frontend-worker` SKILL.md clarifying it should not be assigned to doc-only tasks) would avoid this mismatch for future missions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false with deviations for steps 4 and 6. Transcript shows worker invoked `frontend-worker` skill then ran only backend validators (`pytest`, `ruff`, `mypy`), not `npm run build` or `svelte-check`.

summary: Feature passes review. All six YPM_HOST references in README.md and the one in traefik-proxy/README.md were correctly replaced with VIRTUAL_HOST, with no unintended changes to surrounding text or formatting. Post-commit verification confirms zero YPM_HOST occurrences remain. The only observation is a skill assignment mismatch (frontend-worker used for a doc-only task), which the worker handled correctly but warrants a note for future mission planning.
